text: /c118Eat plenty of vegetables and fruits. /c118Avoid superstitions and food taboos. /c118Do not use alcohol and tobacco. Take medicines only when prescribed. /c118Take iron, folate and calcium supplements regularly, after 14-16 weeks of pregnancy and continue the same during lactation. /c63Folic acid is essential for the synthesis of haemoglobin. /c63Folic acid deficiency leads to macrocytic anaemia. /c63Pregnant women need more of folic acid. /c63Folic acid supplements increase birth weight and reduce congenital anomalies. /c63Green leafy vegetables, legumes, nuts and liver are good sources of folic acid. /c63500 mg (0.5mg) folic acid supplementation is advised preconceptionally and through out pregnancy for women with history of congenital anomalies (neural tube defects, cleft palate).
